indicators.statement: State the meaning and types of festivals 5.1: Ability to combine information the relevance of, and the and ideas from several sources to reach a
exemplars: need to participate in, conclusion. religious festivals 1. Brainstorm and explain the meaning of festivals. 5.6: Demonstrate a thorough understanding

Festivals are special occasions at which rituals are performed of a generalised concept or facts to task or to honour and give thanks to God and other spirit forces for situation. their past favours and make request for more favours. They are occasions to remember past important events.
Identify and explain the types of festivals in Ghana. Types of Festivals
Agricultural Festivals: Celebrated to thank God, the gods and ancestors for a good harvest and ask for more in the coming year. Examples are Bakatue (Elmina) and Yam Festival (Tedudu - Peki)

Ancestral/Historical Festivals: Celebrated in remembrance of the past deeds of heroes and heroines and to remember past events in the life of the community. Examples are Odwira (Akuapem), Bugumchugu (Dagomba/Mamprusi) and Hogbetsotsoza (Anlo); Christmas and Eid-Adha.

B7-B9 CI5: KNOWLEDGE, UNDERSTANDING, SKILLS AND CI6: REFLECTION AND EVALUATION STRATEGIES 5.1: Examine alternatives in creating new things 6.1: Exhibit strong memory, intuitive thinking, and respond appropriately 5.2: Ability to merge simple/complex ideas to create novel situations or 6.2: Ability to reflect on approaches to creative tasks and evaluate the things effectiveness of tools used 5.3: Identification of requirements of a given situation and justification of 6.3: Ability to select the most effective creative tools for work, and give more than one creative tool that will be suitable reasons for the choice 5.4: Ability to visualise alternatives, see possibilities, and identify problems 6.4: Imagining and seeing things in a different way and challenges 5.5: Ability to try new alternatives and different approaches 6.5: Anticipate and overcome difficulties relating to taking initiatives 5.6: Understand and use analogies and metaphors 6.6: Being open-minded, adapting and modifying ideas to achieve creative results 5.7: Putting forward constructive comments, ideas, explanations and new 6.7: Look and think about things differently and from different ways of doing things perspectives 6.8: Recognise and generalise information and experience; search for trends and patterns 6.9: Interpret and apply learning in new contexts 6.10: Reflect on work and explore the thinking behind thoughts and processes

B7-B9 DL5: PHOTO-VISUAL AND INFORMATION LITERACY DL6: SOCIO-EMOTIONAL AND REPRODUCTION LITERACY DL5.1: Ability to ascertain when information is needed and be able to 6.1: Understand the sociological and emotional aspects of cyberspace identify, locate, evaluate and effectively use it to solve a problem DL5.2: Ability to recognise and avoid traps in cyberspace 6.2: Create a meaningful and original piece of work, or its interpretation by integrating existing information DL5.3: Ability to find and utilise digital content DL6.3: Use digital tools to create novel things DL5.4: Ability to construct knowledge from a non-linear hyper-textual DL6.4: Adhere to behavioural protocols that prevail in cyberspace navigation DL5.5: Evaluate the quality and validity of information DL6.5: Recognition of societal issues emanating from the use of digital technologies DL5.6: Preparedness to make better decisions using available information DL6.6: Knowledge and recognition of ethical use of information Please note these inclusivity issues The core competencies outlined in this document must be assessed taking into consideration learners with special needs (physical disabilities, learning disabilities, etc.). Consider the use of realia for visual and visually challenged learners. A system of creating alternatives for tasks must also be adopted. BIBLIOGRAPHY
